---
title: "Analyze Sales"
author: "Rutuja Jadhav"
output:
  pdf_document: default
---



```{r AivenConnect, echo=FALSE,  message=FALSE}

# load required library
library(RMySQL)
library(kableExtra)

# CONNECT TO AIVEN
# define settings
db_host_aiven <- "YOUR_HOST"
db_port_aiven <- 00000
db_name_aiven <- "YOUR_DB_NAME"
db_user_aiven <- "YOUR_USERNAME"
db_pwd_aiven <- "YOUR_PASSWORD"

# embedded SSL certificate
db_cert <- 
  '
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
xxxx
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
'

# connect securely to remote MySQL server and database
mydb.aiven <-  dbConnect(RMySQL::MySQL(), 
                         user = db_user_aiven, 
                         password = db_pwd_aiven,
                         dbname = db_name_aiven, 
                         host = db_host_aiven, 
                         port = db_port_aiven,
                         sslmode = "require",
                         sslcert = db_cert)
```

## Analysis by Restaurant

```{r analysisByRestaurant, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
restaurant_analysis <- dbGetQuery(mydb.aiven, "
  SELECT r.restaurantName AS Restaurant,
         COUNT(v.visitID) AS TotalVisits,
         COUNT(DISTINCT v.customerID) AS UniqueCustomers,
         SUM(CASE WHEN c.loyaltyMember = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS LoyaltyCustomers,
         ROUND(SUM(b.foodBill + b.alcoholBill), 2) AS TotalRevenue
  FROM restaurants r
  LEFT JOIN visit v ON r.restaurantID = v.restaurantID
  LEFT JOIN bill b ON v.visitID = b.visitID
  LEFT JOIN customers c ON v.customerID = c.customerID
  GROUP BY r.restaurantID, r.restaurantName
  ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC
")

kable(restaurant_analysis,
      col.names = c("Restaurant", "Total Visits", "Unique Customers", 
                    "Loyalty Customers", "Total Revenue ($)"),
      caption = "Analysis by Restaurant") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "hold_position", "scale_down"))
```

\newpage

## Sales and Visit Analytics

```{r salesVisitAnalytics, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# dynamically calculate prior 3 years
current_year <- as.integer(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y"))
start_year <- current_year - 3

sales_analytics <- dbGetQuery(mydb.aiven, sprintf("
  SELECT r.restaurantName AS Restaurant,
         ROUND(SUM(b.foodBill * (1 - b.foodDiscountPercentage) + b.alcoholBill), 2) AS TotalRevenue,
         ROUND(SUM(b.foodBill + b.alcoholBill) / COUNT(v.visitID), 2) AS AvgPerParty,
         ROUND(AVG(v.partySize), 2) AS AvgPartySize
  FROM restaurants r
  LEFT JOIN visit v ON r.restaurantID = v.restaurantID
  LEFT JOIN bill b ON v.visitID = b.visitID
  WHERE YEAR(v.visitDate) > %d
  GROUP BY r.restaurantID, r.restaurantName
  ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC
", start_year))

kable(sales_analytics,
      col.names = c("Restaurant", "Total Revenue ($)", "Avg Per Party ($)", "Avg Party Size"),
      caption = "Sales and Visit Analytics (Prior 3 Years)") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "hold_position", "scale_down"))
```

\newpage

## Trend by Month for Florida

```{r trendByMonth, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# dynamically calculate most recent two years
current_year <- as.integer(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y"))
year1 <- current_year - 1
year2 <- current_year - 2

# query monthly revenue for Florida restaurants for most recent two years
florida_trend <- dbGetQuery(mydb.aiven, sprintf("
  SELECT MONTH(v.visitDate) AS Month,
         YEAR(v.visitDate) AS Year,
         ROUND(SUM(b.foodBill * (1 - b.foodDiscountPercentage) + b.alcoholBill), 2) AS Revenue
  FROM visit v
  JOIN bill b ON v.visitID = b.visitID
  JOIN restaurants r ON v.restaurantID = r.restaurantID
  JOIN city c ON r.cityID = c.cityID
  WHERE c.state = 'FL'
  AND YEAR(v.visitDate) IN (%d, %d)
  GROUP BY YEAR(v.visitDate), MONTH(v.visitDate)
  ORDER BY Month, Year
", year1, year2))

# calculate average revenue per month across the two years
avg_revenue <- tapply(florida_trend$Revenue, florida_trend$Month, mean)
months <- as.integer(names(avg_revenue))
month_names <- month.abb[months]

# plot line chart
plot(months, avg_revenue,
     type = "b",
     pch = 16,
     col = "steelblue",
     xlab = "Month",
     ylab = "Average Revenue ($)",
     main = paste("Average Monthly Revenue in Florida\n(", year2, "-", year1, ")"),
     xaxt = "n",
     ylim = c(0, max(avg_revenue) * 1.2))

# add month labels on x axis
axis(1, at = months, labels = month_names)

# add data labels
text(months, avg_revenue, 
     labels = format(round(avg_revenue), big.mark = ","),
     pos = 3, cex = 0.7)
```


```{r disconnectAiven, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# disconnect
dbDisconnect(mydb.aiven)
```
